content.problemStatement:
Recently, Chef found a new formula for computing powers of a sequence:


You are given two sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and (l1,r1),(l2,r2),…,(lN,rN)(l_1, r_1), (l_2, r_2), \ldots, (l_N, r_N)(l1​,r1​),(l2​,r2​),…,(lN​,rN​).
The 111-st power of the sequence AAA is A1=AA^1 = AA1=A.
For each k>1k \gt 1k>1, the kkk-th power of the sequence AAA (denoted by AkA^kAk) is a sequence with length NNN such that for each valid iii, the iii-th element of this sequence is (Ak)i=(Ak−1)li⊕(Ak−1)li+1⊕…⊕(Ak−1)ri−1⊕(Ak−1)ri .(A^k)_i = (A^{k-1})_{l_i} \oplus (A^{k-1})_{l_i+1} \oplus \ldots \oplus (A^{k-1})_{r_i-1} \oplus (A^{k-1})_{r_i} \,.(Ak)i​=(Ak−1)li​​⊕(Ak−1)li​+1​⊕…⊕(Ak−1)ri​−1​⊕(Ak−1)ri​​.
After discovering this new formula, Chef tried to find AKA^KAK, but he felt that it is very time consuming. Therefore, Chef wants you to do it. Can you find the KKK-th power of AAA for Chef?
constraints: 1≤li≤ri≤N1 \le l_i \le r_i \le N1≤li​≤ri​≤N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili​ and rir_iri​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers (AK)1,(AK)2,…,(AK)N(A^K)_1, (A^K)_2, \ldots, (A^K)_N(AK)1​,(AK)2​,…,(AK)N​.
